Basics.Objective: Avoid taking penalty cards. The player with the lowest score when someone reaches 100 points wins.

Suits.Clubs: No penalty points. The 2 of Clubs always leads the first trick of each round.
Suits.Diamonds: No penalty points. A safe suit for dumping when you can't follow.
Suits.Spades: Contains the Queen of Spades, worth 13 penalty points. Be careful when following suit.
Suits.Hearts: Every Heart is worth 1 penalty point. Hearts cannot be led until 'broken' — a Heart is played on another suit's trick.

Basics.First Trick: The player holding the 2 of Clubs leads the first trick. No penalty cards (Hearts or Queen of Spades) may be played on the first trick unless you have no other option.
Basics.Following Suit: You must play a card of the led suit if you have one. If you have none, you may play any card — including penalty cards.

Winning a Trick.Standard Rule: The highest card of the led suit wins the trick. Cards of other suits cannot win, regardless of rank. Aces are high, 2s are low.

Red Joker.description: Counts as Hearts or Diamonds. Always wins the trick regardless of rank or suit.

Black Joker.description: Counts as Spades or Clubs. Always wins the trick regardless of rank or suit.

The Joker.description: When leading, always wins the trick — beats everything, including other Jokers. When not leading, always loses. Counts as any suit, so it can be played anytime.

The Crown.description: The highest rank card played in the trick wins, regardless of suit. Overrides the normal lead-suit rule.

The Roulette.description: The trick winner is chosen randomly among the players.

The Binding.description: Forces the trick winner to also win the next trick. Only a Joker can override this.

Passing & Dealer.The Dealer: The player who scored the least points in the previous round becomes the dealer. The dealer chooses the pass direction and how many cards to pass.
Passing & Dealer.Direction: The dealer picks one: Left, Right, Across, or No Passing.
Passing & Dealer.Card Count: The dealer picks how many cards each player passes: 2, 3, or 4. If No Passing is chosen, the round skips straight to the contract draft.
Passing & Dealer.Strategy: Pass high-risk cards like the Queen of Spades or high Hearts. Keep low cards for safe following.

Contracts & Draft.What Are Contracts: Contracts are secret missions for the round. Each contract has a specific goal to achieve during play. Completing your contract earns you a Transmutation card as a reward. Failing has no extra penalty.
Contracts & Draft.The Draft: Before each round, players draft contracts in a 3-pick snake draft. You pick one contract from the available options, then the remaining contracts pass to the next player. This repeats 3 times.

Contract Tiers.Easy (Tier 0): Simple goals like avoiding hearts, winning tricks, or collecting suits. Examples: Heartless, Trick Master, Clean Hands.
Contract Tiers.Medium (Tier 1): Harder goals involving timing or passed cards. Examples: Fast Start, Return to Sender, Bookends.
Contract Tiers.Hard (Tier 2): High-risk, high-reward goals. Examples: Shoot the Moon, Moon Guard, Trailblazer, Alchemist's Strike.

Contracts & Draft.Rewards: Each contract is paired with a random Transmutation card. Complete the contract to add that Transmutation to your inventory.

Transmutations.How to Use: Drag a Transmutation card from your inventory onto a card in your hand to transform it. The transmuted card keeps its suit-following behavior but gains a special effect. The 2 of Clubs and Queen of Spades cannot be transmuted.
Transmutations.Persistence: Transmutation cards persist in your inventory across rounds until used. Build your collection by completing contracts each round.

Red Joker.description: Counts as Hearts or Diamonds. Always wins the trick. Worth 0 points.

Black Joker.description: Counts as Spades or Clubs. Always wins the trick. Worth 0 points.

The Joker.description: When leading, beats everything including other Jokers. When not leading, always loses. Counts as any suit. Worth 0 points.

Shadow Queen.description: Transforms a card into a second Queen of Spades — worth 13 penalty points.

The Pendulum.description: The transmuted card scores -1 instead of its normal point value. Reduces the trick winner's score.

The Gatherer.description: The trick winner reduces their round score by 3 points. Applied at the end of the round.

The Trap.description: If played in the same trick as the Queen of Spades, the Queen's penalty is doubled from 13 to 26 points.

The Inversion.description: All penalty points in this trick become negative (reduce score). Score-reducing effects like Gatherer and Pendulum become additions instead.

The Curse.description: The trick winner must lead the next trick with Hearts, even if Hearts haven't been broken yet.

The Anchor.description: The lead suit of this trick becomes the forced lead suit for the next trick.

The Rogue.description: The trick winner reveals one of an opponent's hand cards to all players.

The Shield.description: The trick winner scores 0 points for the next 3 tricks.

The Martyr.description: The trick winner's round score is doubled at the end of the round. Multiple Martyrs stack: 2 = x4, 3 = x8.

The Parasite.description: All penalty points from this trick go to the player who played the Parasite, not the trick winner.

The Bounty.description: If the Queen of Spades is in this trick, the player who played her takes the 13 points — not the trick winner.

The Fog.description: The transmuted card is hidden — no player can see what it is until it's played. The card's rules and effects still apply normally.

The Mirror.description: Copies the effect of the last transmutation card played in any previous trick this round.

Scoring.Points: Each Heart = 1 point. Queen of Spades = 13 points. All other cards = 0. The goal is to have the lowest score.
Scoring.Shooting the Moon: If one player collects ALL penalty cards (every Heart and the Queen of Spades), they score 0 for the round and every other player receives 26 points instead. Transmutations can change individual point values, but the moon is about collecting all the penalty cards.
Scoring.Game Over: The game ends when any player reaches 100 total points. The player with the lowest score wins.
